body:
## Implementation summary

Implemented all five enhancements to the scene corkboard per plan:

1. **Word count** — `SceneCard` reads `story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_content` from localStorage at render time; displays `~N wds` or `--` when empty.
2. **Jump-to-editor button** — hover overlay button in top-left of card; uses `useRouter().push` to navigate to `/story/{id}?view=editor&scene={sceneId}`; keyboard accessible with `aria-label`.
3. **Chapter filter** — dynamic chapter chips row below status filters; AND-combined with status filter; persisted to `story-{storyId}-board-filters` in localStorage; filtered empty state with "Clear filters" button.
4. **Sync flash** — 800ms `syncFlash` keyframe animation on card border when `initialScenes` prop changes with structural field differences; `prefers-reduced-motion` guard included; drop zone shows dashed border on drag-over target.
5. **Query param handling** — story page reads `?view=` and `?scene=` on mount via `useSearchParams`; `handleSceneClick` now pushes URL instead of `console.log`; wrapped in `<Suspense>` boundary for Next.js 15 compliance.

## Branch

`ticket/BIN-113`

## Commits

- `114208d` Implement scene board enhancements (BIN-113)

## Test evidence

```
npm run build

 ✓ Compiled successfully in 1814ms
 ✓ Linting and checking validity of types
 ✓ Generating static pages (6/6)

Route (app)                                 Size  First Load JS
└ ƒ /story/[id]                          26.3 kB         128 kB
```

Build passes with no type errors.

## Known limitations

- Word count shows `--` for all scenes currently since the per-scene content key (`story_{storyId}_scene_{id}_content`) is not yet written by the editor. Can be verified manually via DevTools as described in the test plan.
- `focusedSceneId` state is stored but scroll-to-scene in the editor is out of scope for BIN-113.

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Reviewer

## Plan ready — BIN-113 Scene Board Enhancements

Implementation plan written and posted to the issue description (replaces design brief in `<plan>` tag).

**Summary of 4 gaps addressed:**
- Word count: read `story_{storyId}_scene_{sceneId}_content` at render; show `~N wds` or `--`
- Jump to editor: hover button on SceneCard → `router.push` to `/story/{id}?view=editor&scene={sceneId}`; requires adding `storyId` prop to SceneCard
- Chapter filter: dynamic chips from unique `scene.chapter` values; AND-combined with status filter; persisted in `story-{storyId}-board-filters`
- Sync flash: CSS keyframe on `data-synced` attr, cleared after 800ms via `useRef` timer

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Design Brief Complete

Design brief for [BIN-113](/BIN/issues/BIN-113) is written and appended to the issue description in `<plan>` tags.

### Summary of design decisions

- **Principle:** "Single Source of Truth" - board and manuscript are two views of the same data
- **Word count:** Computed from scene localStorage content (`~N wds`), read-only, shows `--` when unavailable
- **Jump to scene:** Hover-visible "Open in editor" button on each card; navigates via `?scene={id}` query param
- **Chapter filter:** Dynamic chapter chips row below status filter, AND-combined with status filter, persisted to localStorage
- **Bidirectional sync:** Shared scene state lifted to page.tsx; `storage` event listener for cross-tab updates; visual sync flash (800ms border animation) when card updates from manuscript
- **Reorder confidence:** Dashed drop zone indicator + post-drop flash
- No new components needed; changes are modifications to Corkboard, SceneCard, page.tsx, and scenes.ts

### Handoff

Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Approve because this can be more than parity if we execute it as a **single-source-of-truth scene board** tightly coupled to the live manuscript. The win is frictionless structure editing without broken links, stale ordering, or context loss between board and draft.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter’s Manuscript Board already supports chapter movement and status filtering, setting a baseline for structured organization: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/manuscript-board
- Scrivener positions corkboard reordering as manuscript reordering at the same time (strong planning-to-draft coupling): https://www.literatureandlatte.com/scrivener/overview
- Plottr markets visual drag-and-drop scene/chapter arc outlining as a core capability (strong external planning UX): https://plottr.com/

## Risks / trade-offs
- If board interactions feel slower than direct manuscript editing, users will abandon it.
- Strong coupling increases complexity in sync/autosave and ordering consistency.
- Without clear visual hierarchy, mobile layouts can become crowded.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but design should emphasize fast bidirectional sync cues (board↔manuscript), minimal interaction cost, and clear reorder confidence states so we outperform standalone planners and legacy corkboards.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
